title: Menu Preview Polish and Arrow-Key Scroll Fix
content:
## Overview

Commit `b7b116ef` on `chores/cleanup-bugs` addressed five related cleanup items: upgrading the landing-page snake preview to match the real game's visual quality, removing two vestigial bot-control surfaces from the game screen, preventing arrow keys from scrolling the browser window during play, and fixing a bot loop-guard bug that caused escape mode to never terminate.

---

## Task 1 — Landing-page preview parity (`src/menu-demo.ts`, `index.html`, `src/style.css`)

### Head and tail graphics

The demo previously drew every snake segment as a plain identical block. The real game's head and tail rendering has been ported into `src/menu-demo.ts`:

- **`drawPrism(corners, bh, top, right, left)`** — shared extrusion helper extracted so both standard body blocks and the tapered tail reuse the same drawing path. The existing `drawBlock` delegates to it and accepts an optional `heightScale` parameter (default `1`).
- **`drawSnakeHead(gx, gy, colors, dir)`** — renders a taller block (`HEAD_HEIGHT_SCALE = 1.6`) with two eyes on the leading face.
- **`drawSnakeTail(gx, gy, colors, awayDir)`** + **`tailDirection(snake)`** + **`vectorToDirection(v)`** — tapered tail prism pointing in the trailing direction (`TAIL_HEIGHT_SCALE = 0.7`).

Segment classification in `draw()`: `index === 0 → head`, `index === length-1 → tail`, otherwise `body`. A length-1 snake is treated as head-only (no tail). Color tuples match the main game: head `['#4ade80','#22c55e','#16a34a']`, body `['#22c55e','#16a34a','#15803d']`, food `['#ef4444','#dc2626','#b91c1c']`.

### Iron Snake / classic board selection

- `generateIronSnakeBoard` is imported from `./iron-snake`; a `boardMask: Uint8Array | null` field tracks the current board shape.
- An `onBoard(x, y)` helper checks rectangle bounds and, when `boardMask` is set, `boardMask[y*gridSize+x] === 1`. `inBounds` delegates to it, so bot pathfinding, collision detection, and food spawning all respect the irregular shape automatically.
- **Board type is decided once per page load**, not per life. This ensures a given visit consistently showcases one style (Iron Snake or classic rectangle) across all lives. Visitors who reload see the other style. The rationale: at a 50/50 per-life split, screenshots almost always landed on the classic board because the snake survives much longer on the roomy rectangle.
- On each life reset (`resetSnake()`), a fresh board is generated for the chosen style. If `generateIronSnakeBoard` returns `null`, the demo falls back to classic (`boardMask = null`). `DEMO_GRID_SIZE` is set to ~14 to give irregular shapes adequate room (Iron Snake `MIN_GRID` is 12).
- The snake spawns at the on-board cell nearest center (center may be void on an irregular board).
- `spawnFood()` only considers on-board empty cells.
- `drawGroundPlane`, `drawGridLines`, and `drawGroundBorder` all have masked branches that skip off-board cells and trace the true irregular outline for Iron boards, with full-rectangle branches retained for classic.

### Random bot and corner label

- On every life reset, a bot is chosen at random from `AVAILABLE_BOTS` (replaces the fixed `DEFAULT_BOT_ID`).
- **`index.html`**: a `<div id="menu-demo-bot-label" class="menu-demo-bot-label">` element is added inside `.menu-hero`.
- **`src/style.css`**: the label is styled as a small translucent dark pill with a green border, absolutely positioned in the top-left corner of the hero container. Text rendering stays in the DOM (not on the canvas) for crispness at all resolutions and accessibility.
- `MenuDemo` queries `#menu-demo-bot-label` once in the constructor and updates its text (e.g. `Bot: <name>`) in `resetSnake()`.

---

## Task 2 — Remove the "Disable Bot" button (`index.html`, `src/main.ts`)

The `#toggle-bot` button and its `B` keyboard shortcut were vestigial — bot play moved to the pre-game Solo Bot menu panel.

**Removed:**
- `#toggle-bot` button element from `index.html`; "Press B to toggle bot" from the controls hint text.
- Click listener, `B`-key branch, and `toggleBot()` method from `src/main.ts`.
- The null-guard in `updateBotUI()` that referenced the deleted button element (leaving it would have silently stopped the bot status display from updating).
- `toggleBot` lookup and its `.hidden` toggle from `updateModeUI()`; "Press B to toggle bot" from the single-player controls string.

**Kept intact:** `botEnabled` flag, `botEverActive` leaderboard-disqualification flag, the Solo Bot launch path that sets `botEnabled = true`, human-input suppression that reads the flag, and the leaderboard check that uses `botEverActive`.

---

## Task 3 — Remove mid-game bot switching (`index.html`, `src/main.ts`, `src/style.css`)

The `#game-bot-selection` dropdown on the live game screen allowed swapping the driving bot mid-game, inconsistent with pre-game bot selection panels.

**Removed:**
- `#game-bot-selection` block (`#game-bot-select` + `#game-bot-description`) from `index.html`.
- `gameSelect` from the `botSelectors` array in `setupBotSelectors()`; `'game-bot-description'` from `bot1Ids` in `updateBotDescriptions()`; `gameBotSelection` lookup and its `.hidden` toggle from `updateModeUI()` — all in `src/main.ts`.
- Dead `.bot-selection` CSS rules and their responsive variant from `src/style.css`.

Bot selection is now exclusively a pre-game action via the Solo Bot and Bot vs. Bot menu panels. The existing `#bot-status` display on the game screen still shows the active bot's name for Solo Bot runs.

---

## Task 4 — Arrow keys scroll the page (`src/main.ts`)

On short or narrow viewports where the page overflowed, pressing arrow keys to move the snake also scrolled the browser window.

**Fix:** At the top of `handleKeyPress` in `src/main.ts`, immediately after the existing guard that exempts `INPUT`/`TEXTAREA`/`SELECT` elements, an explicit `e.preventDefault()` is called for `ArrowUp`, `ArrowDown`, `ArrowLeft`, and `ArrowRight`. The prevention is unconditional across all game states (not just when a game is active) to avoid any simultaneous scroll+move scenario. WASD is unaffected. Typing in forms is unaffected because the text-field guard runs first.

---

## Loop-guard fix (`src/bots/loop-guard.ts`)

The bot loop-guard escape mode never terminated.

**Root cause:** The escape timer was reset to its full value on every tick that a valid escape move existed, so it could never count down to zero. Additionally, when the escape window did close, the record of recently visited positions was not cleared, causing the bot to immediately re-detect the same stale loop and restart escape mode.

**Fix:** The state machine now has two independent phases:
1. **Start an escape** — only triggered on a fresh loop detection when no escape is already in progress.
2. **Continue an escape** — countdown runs for a fixed number of ticks without being reset.

When the countdown reaches zero, the visit history is cleared (giving the bot a clean slate) and the bot's own direction is restored. The guard checks whether an escape is already running before starting a new one, making the two phases independent and the duration predictable.
